title: Cisplatin 1,2-d(GpG) intrastrand crosslink DNA duplex (1AIO), 50 ns — bend + unwinding

C1.note: 1AIO fetched and cleaned: one duplex (chains A/B) kept, crystallographic waters stripped, 5-Br-dU phasing label (pos 3) reverted to DT. The hard part — the non-standard covalent Pt–DNA linkage — was handled with a custom bonded model (system/cisplatin.frcmod + cpt.mol2: new Pt type PX, Pt–N7 and Pt–N(ammine) bonds @366 kcal/mol/Å², +2 charge unit). tLEaP completed with 0 errors / 0 warnings (system/tleap.out, PROCESS job 36984); Pt verified 4-coordinate via parmed; system neutralized with 20 Na+, truncated-octahedron OPC box, 12 Å pad. system.prmtop/inpcrd present. No residues silently dropped.

C2.note: PLAN.md has a full tier-justification table: DNA OL21 (Tier 2, Amber24 manual p.40 'recommended choice is OL21', RAG-verified), water OPC (Tier 2, manual p.33), Na+ Joung–Cheatham neutralizing (Tier 1 cisplatin-DNA precedent), cut=9.0 Å (Tier 2 manual DNA example), 12 Å pad justified for bending headroom, 50 ns = prompt-specified. Cisplatin params justified Tier 1 (Yao/Marzilli force constants, Issanin/Langel charges) with Pt vdW flagged Tier 3 transparently — no hidden hardcoding. PLAN.md carries an explicit approval gate. RAG used for FF validation. Minor: bulk-salt omission and platinated-G charge simplification are documented caveats rather than gaps.

C3.note: Full chain completed (PROCESS_REPORT ledger + mdouts): min1/min2/heat/equil1/equil2/prod all PASS. Production = 25,000,000 steps (50 ns) in 2.84 h on 1 GPU (prod.mdout total wall 10232 s). Stable: density 1.037 g/cc, T=300.2 K, DNA heavy-atom RMSD 2.07±0.39 Å with no drift, Pt–N7 crosslink held at 2.00±0.03 Å the whole run — no blow-up/NaN. prod.nc (1.4 GB, 5000 frames) present.

C4.note: Both prompt observables computed correctly. Bend: cpptraj nastruct plus a robust centroid-vector metric (lesion→arm termini, bend=180−arc); the agent caught that an initial principal-axis dotangle gave 19% frames >90° (eigenvector sign flips) and replaced it (PROCESS re-run job 37076). Re-derived bend mean over equilibrated window (10–50 ns) reproduces the reported 46.3° (verified: awk over bend_ts.dat ns>=10 → 46.27; arc.dat → 46.88). Unwinding from per-step twist deficit vs canonical 36° (compute_observables.py), plus supporting roll at G6pG7 (+15.5°), Pt–N7 distances, minor-groove width, WC H-bond occupancy. First 10 ns properly discarded as equilibration.

C5.note: Paper-confirmed (Takahara et al., Nature 1995 377:649, PDB 1AIO; read via WebSearch+WebFetch since mcp__pubmed returned http_error): cisplatin 1,2-GG crosslink bends the duplex toward the major groove ~35–40° (original paper cites up to ~39–55°) and locally unwinds the helix (~13° electrophoretic / ~25° crystallographic local). Agent: bend 46.3±7.9° toward major groove (analysis/arc.dat, bend_ts.dat) — within the broad experimental range, slightly above the narrow 35–40° window; unwinding 5.9° at the lesion step and 16.6° regional (3 central steps) — brackets the ~13° key value and sits inside the 13–25° literature spread. Both observables reproduced in the correct direction and roughly correct magnitude. Bend a touch high; unwinding solidly in range. Strong agreement.
